stack: Markdown documentation and export test files

handoverSummary: AMatriz v1.0.0 remains complete under the MIT License. Copyright ownership has been updated consistently to Copyright (c) 2026 Electritects Pty Ltd in LICENSE, README, specifications, CSS headers, gallery staging post/CSS, installed Typora CSS copies, and the validation document conclusion. Packaged CSS, installed Typora copies, and gallery staging copies are synchronized.

keyAchievements.title: Theme source established
keyAchievements.description: coding/amatriz.css remains the source of truth for the dark AMatriz theme.

keyAchievements.title: Dark PDF export corrected
keyAchievements.description: amatriz.css preserves the v1.0.0 dark PDF export behavior.

keyAchievements.title: PDF export test document rebuilt
keyAchievements.description: The test document is now docs/Typora Test File v1.0.0.md and includes white-print, black-text, and inline HTML color preservation checks.

keyAchievements.title: White printing theme added
keyAchievements.description: Added coding/amatriz-print-white.css as a Typora-compliant lowercase, hyphenated theme variant with white PDF output.

keyAchievements.title: Prior next-step checklist cleared
keyAchievements.description: The v1.0.0 follow-up checklist was addressed for the v1.0.0 handoff: source files were kept in coding/, export behavior was re-specified, docs were updated, and version telemetry was updated through this handoff file.

keyAchievements.title: Print validation CSS stabilized
keyAchievements.description: Installed Typora theme CSS was adjusted for table splitting with repeated headers, single-panel fenced code blocks, heading top clipping protection, Mermaid containment, and readable sky-blue samples.

keyAchievements.title: A4 validation document expanded
keyAchievements.description: docs/Typora Test File v1.0.0.md now exercises A4 page flow, long tables, math, constants, chemistry notation, Mermaid diagrams, long lists, Unicode scripts, checklist states, and footnotes.

keyAchievements.title: Version rollback completed
keyAchievements.description: Project identifiers, documentation filenames, PDF filenames, render image filenames, and telemetry were rolled back to v1.0.0/v100 without marking any version completed.

keyAchievements.title: Final validation document and print polish completed
keyAchievements.description: The installed Typora themes and v1.0.0 validation document were refined for 17-page dark and white PDF exports, including compact print tables, cleaned outline structure, hidden print-only footnote backlinks, expanded Chemistry content, Unicode verb samples, and README guidance for screen versus hardcopy use.

keyAchievements.title: v1.0.0 release package finalized
keyAchievements.description: Final validated CSS was copied into coding/, release docs and specifications were synchronized, MIT licensing was added, Typora Theme Gallery submission staging files were prepared, and the project was marked complete for v1.0.0.

nextSteps: Re-export docs/Typora Test File v1.0.0.md with AMatriz and AMatriz Print White after the document-independent CSS cleanup.
Publish this repository or a v1.0.0 release ZIP to GitHub so the Typora gallery post can reference stable homepage and download URLs.
Replace the placeholder homepage and download URLs in typora-theme-gallery-submission/_posts/theme/2026-06-01-amatriz.md.
Copy the staged post, thumbnail, preview media, and CSS files into a fork of typora/theme.typora.io and open the Typora Theme Gallery pull request.
blockers: No local code, packaging, documentation, or license blockers remain for v1.0.0. External submission still requires a GitHub remote/release URL and a user-authenticated pull request to the Typora Theme Gallery. Because the validation Markdown now includes a copyright line, re-export the AMatriz PDFs in Typora before PR submission if those PDFs are part of the release package.
comments: To submit a custom theme to the Typora Theme Gallery, fork the Typora Themes GitHub repository and submit a pull request.Follow these steps to prepare and submit your theme:Prepare your theme: Ensure your theme's CSS file uses only lowercase letters and hyphens (e.g., my-custom-theme.css).Create a thumbnail: Make a 250$\times$200 px thumbnail image and place it in the thumbnails directory.Add a post: Create a new markdown post in the _posts directory of your forked repository and fill out the relevant YAML fields.Test & Submit: Test your theme in Typora, then push your changes to your fork and open a pull request on the Typora Theme Gallery GitHub Repository.For detailed styling guidelines and step-by-step instructions, check out the Typora Submit a Theme Documentation.
